examples.title: Algorithm: Infix to Postfix Conversion
examples.description: Converts an infix expression to postfix notation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM InfixToPostfix(infixExpression)
INPUT: infixExpression (string)
OUTPUT: postfixExpression (string)

BEGIN
 operatorStack = new Stack()
 outputString = ""

 FOR EACH character IN infixExpression
 IF character IS operand
 ADD character TO outputString
 ELSE IF character IS opening parenthesis
 PUSH character ONTO operatorStack
 ELSE IF character IS closing parenthesis
 WHILE TOP OF operatorStack IS NOT opening parenthesis
 POP operator FROM operatorStack
 ADD operator TO outputString
 POP opening parenthesis FROM operatorStack
 ELSE IF character IS operator
 WHILE operatorStack IS NOT EMPTY AND TOP OF operatorStack HAS HIGHER PRECEDENCE THAN character
 POP operator FROM operatorStack
 ADD operator TO outputString
 PUSH character ONTO operatorStack

 WHILE operatorStack IS NOT EMPTY
 POP operator FROM operatorStack
 ADD operator TO outputString

 RETURN outputString
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm uses a stack to keep track of operators and their precedence. It iterates through the infix expression, adding operands to the output string and pushing operators onto the stack. When it encounters a closing parenthesis, it pops operators from the stack and adds them to the output string until it finds the matching opening parenthesis.

examples.title: Python: Infix to Postfix Conversion
examples.description: Converts an infix expression to postfix notation
examples.explanation: This Python function implements the infix to postfix conversion algorithm using a list as a stack. It iterates through the infix expression, adding operands to the output string and pushing operators onto the stack. When it encounters a closing parenthesis, it pops operators from the stack and adds them to the output string until it finds the matching opening parenthesis.

examples.title: C: Evaluate Postfix Expression
examples.explanation: This C function evaluates a postfix expression using a stack. It iterates through the postfix expression, pushing operands onto the stack and popping them when it encounters an operator. It then applies the operator to the two operands and pushes the result back onto the stack.
